title: WHI-14f: Fix window apply — ticks not loading into inspector, scrubber stuck on old window
description: CRITICAL: Window trimmer Apply still broken. Symptom: toolbar badge shows correct first tick ID of selected window, but scrubber/time travel stays on the old window. Fetched ticks are NOT being ingested into the TreeInspector or the scrubber range is not updating. Need deep investigation of the full data flow: Apply → seekToRange → fetch from server → ingest into TickStore/TreeInspector → update scrubber range. Server + demo agent already running — use yarn ui + Playwright to debug live.
closeReason: Merged: fixed window apply — clearTicks on seek + re-clone pausedInspector

title: fix: seekToRange silently dropped when streaming poll in-flight
description:
Race condition in use-tick-poller.ts: seekToRange returns early if fetchingRef.current is true (set by streaming fetchForward poll). Since fetchForward runs every 200ms, there's a high chance seekToRange is silently dropped when user clicks Apply in the WindowRangeTrimmer.

Consequence: user enters paused mode (goToTick fires), manualWindowActive is set (streaming stops), but the range fetch never happens. User is stuck viewing stale data.

Fix approach: seekToRange must preempt in-flight streaming polls. Use a request generation counter: seekToRange increments a seekGenRef and proceeds regardless of fetchingRef. fetchForward's .then() checks if seekGenRef changed during flight and discards its result if so. seekToRange should also reset fetchingRef before starting its own fetch.

Files: packages/studio-ui/src/hooks/use-tick-poller.ts (primary), possibly packages/react/src/hooks/useInspector.ts and BehaviourTreeDebugger.tsx for edge cases.
closeReason: Fixed seekToRange race condition: added seekGenRef generation counter so seekToRange preempts in-flight streaming polls. fetchForward discards stale results in .then() and .catch() when seekGen changes.

title: fix: off-by-one in seekToRange — fromTickId excluded
description:
When user selects window starting at tick 132, the first tick returned is 133. The range query uses inclusive bounds (>=, <=) on the server, so the issue is likely in how fromTickId is computed or passed from the WindowRangeTrimmer. Check fracToTick rounding, the limit calculation (toTickId - fromTickId + 1 vs off-by-one), and the server-side findRange query.

Files: packages/react/src/components/panels/WindowRangeTrimmer.tsx (fracToTick), packages/studio-ui/src/hooks/use-tick-poller.ts (seekToRange limit), packages/studio-server/src/infra/knex/tick-repository.ts (findRange).
closeReason: Merged branch overstory/builder-obo-fix/behavior-tree-ist-f09c

title: feat: tick store size should match selected window size
description:
When user selects a window via WindowRangeTrimmer, the inspector maxTicks should equal the window size (toTickId - fromTickId + 1), not the default ringBufferSize (500). Currently the inspector always uses ringBufferSize=500, so if the user selects a window of 1000 ticks, only 500 survive ingestion. If they select 100, the store is unnecessarily large.

The fix should propagate the selected window size from onFetchTickRange through to the inspector's maxTicks. This likely means:
1. use-studio-controls.ts: expose the active window size (or null when streaming)
2. BehaviourTreeDebugger: when a manual window is active, override inspectorOptions.maxTicks with the window size
3. Alternatively, compute dynamically: when seekToRange is called with (from, to), set a windowMaxTicks = to - from + 1 and expose it alongside isLoadingWindow

Files: packages/studio-ui/src/hooks/use-tick-poller.ts, packages/studio-ui/src/use-studio-controls.ts, packages/react/src/BehaviourTreeDebugger.tsx, packages/studio-ui/src/App.tsx
closeReason: Added windowMaxTicks to StudioControls; propagated from onFetchTickRange through to App.tsx inspectorOptions.maxTicks

title: refactor: simplify time travel window selection — double-fetch race
description:
ROOT CAUSE: handleTrimmerApply in TimelinePanel.tsx calls both onSelectRange(from,to) (→ seekToRange, correct query) and controls.goToTick(from). goToTick checks storedIds which still has the OLD window (seekToRange is async), so tick 'from' is not found → onNeedTick fires → onFetchTicksAround fires a SECOND seekToRange with a much larger window. The second seekToRange bumps seekGenRef, invalidating the first correct response. UI gets the wrong window.

IMMEDIATE FIX: handleTrimmerApply should NOT call goToTick(from) after onSelectRange. Instead, after seekToRange resolves and ticks are loaded, the UI should auto-navigate to the first tick of the new window. The simplest approach: remove goToTick(from) from handleTrimmerApply, and when seekToRange completes (isLoadingWindow transitions false→true→false), auto-set frozenTickId to the oldest tick in the new window.

BROADER CLEANUP: The interaction between goToTick (onNeedTick side-effect), onFetchTicksAround, onFetchTickRange, and seekToRange is unnecessarily layered. Simplify: when a range is explicitly selected, that should be the ONLY fetch. goToTick should only navigate within already-loaded ticks, never trigger fetches during a pending seekToRange.

FILES TO MODIFY:
- packages/react/src/components/panels/TimelinePanel.tsx (handleTrimmerApply — remove goToTick call)
- packages/react/src/BehaviourTreeDebugger.tsx (auto-navigate to first tick after window load completes)
- packages/react/src/hooks/useTimeTravelControls.ts (goToTick should not fire onNeedTick when a seek is in-flight, or expose a navigateOnly variant)
- packages/studio-ui/src/use-studio-controls.ts (review onFetchTicksAround interaction)

ACCEPTANCE: yarn typecheck && yarn lint && yarn test. Selecting a window range via trimmer should fire exactly ONE ticks.range query. After the window loads, the scrubber should automatically navigate to the first tick of the selected range.
closeReason: Merged branch overstory/builder-double-fetch/behavior-tree-ist-41d7

title: fix: start live polling from latest ticks, not tick 0
description:
When entering live mode, the poller starts with afterTickId=0, fetching from the very beginning. Instead it should:
1. Query ticks.bounds to get maxTickId
2. Fetch the last ringBufferSize ticks (from maxTickId - ringBufferSize to maxTickId) as the initial window
3. Then start polling forward from maxTickId

This gives the user an immediate view of recent activity instead of watching ticks load from the start.

FILES: packages/studio-ui/src/hooks/use-tick-poller.ts (fetchForward initial behavior, streaming mode setup)
closeReason: Merged branch overstory/builder-live-seed/behavior-tree-ist-2832

title: cleanup: remove Fetch Batch Size config
description:
The uiSettings.fetchBatchSize config is misleading — the actual polling limit is hardcoded to 100 in fetchForward. The only place fetchBatchSize is used is onFetchTicksAround (for centering a window around a tick), but that behavior is being simplified in the time travel cleanup. Remove the fetchBatchSize field from StudioUiSettings, its UI control in settings, and all references. If onFetchTicksAround still needs a size, use ringBufferSize instead.

FILES: packages/studio-ui/src/hooks/use-ui-settings.ts, packages/studio-ui/src/use-studio-controls.ts, packages/react/src/types.ts (StudioUiSettings), settings UI component
closeReason: Merged branch overstory/builder-remove-batchsize/behavior-tree-ist-13e1

title: bug: windowed time travel shows wrong tick IDs on second window selection
description: When selecting a tick window in windowed mode, the first selection works correctly (exact window displayed). But selecting a SECOND window while already in windowed mode shows wrong tick IDs — the fetch returns correct data from the server, but the UI displays a different range (correct count/size, wrong IDs). Expected: select window → fetch those ticks → new inspector processes exactly those ticks → display. Select another window → repeat same process with fresh state. The inspector/ring-buffer state from the previous window is leaking into the new selection.
closeReason: Superseded by behavior-tree-ist-200d (static TT windows refactor)

title: fix: keep live polling in background during time travel + auto-switch mode on window select
description: Two related mode-switching fixes: (1) Live polling must NEVER stop during time travel. The live buffer should keep polling in the background (separate from time-travel inspector). Returning to live mode should be instant with no reload — just switch back to the live inspector. Currently leaving windowed time travel back to live triggers a full reload from latest ticks. (2) Selecting a window range should automatically switch to time travel/paused mode. Currently if user is in live mode and selects a window, the UI stays in live mode.
closeReason: Merged branch overstory/builder-react-hooks/behavior-tree-ist-200d

title: refactor: static time travel windows — remove incremental append/fetchBefore from TT mode
description: ARCHITECTURE DECISION: Time travel windows must be STATIC. No backward scrolling, no incremental appends, no fetchBefore in TT mode. Two sources of TT windows: (1) Pause from live: copy current live ring buffer as frozen snapshot. (2) Window selector: fetch from server, create new static ring buffer from exactly what was fetched. Live ring buffer ALWAYS polls in background (never affects TT). Exiting TT switches back to live buffer instantly. Remove all fetchBefore/insertTicksBefore/backward-scroll logic from TT path. Incremental diff-ingestion only applies to live ring buffer updates.
closeReason: refactored TT to static windows: removed insertBefore/insertTicksBefore/ingestTicksBefore from core, simplified useTickPoller to live-only (no mode/fetchBefore/seekToRange), added ttWindowTicks state in use-studio-controls for TT window management, removed backward-tick logic from useInspector

title: fix: selecting tick window in live mode should switch UI to paused/time-travel
description: When user selects a tick window via range trimmer while in live mode, navigateToTick(from) sets mode='paused' but the inspector clear during fetch resets totalTicks to 0, triggering useTimeTravelControls effect (line 106) which resets mode back to 'live'. Result: data loads correctly but UI still shows 'live' badge, live button active, no 'Exit Time Travel' button. Fix: ensure mode='paused' survives the inspector-clear cycle during window fetch. Key files: useTimeTravelControls.ts (totalTicks reset effect), BehaviourTreeDebugger.tsx (handleSelectRange + window load effect), TimelinePanel.tsx (handleTrimmerApply).
closeReason: Merged overstory/builder-mode-fix/behavior-tree-ist-c791

title: research: review and answer WHI-9 pivot comment on multiref variant
description: User proposes pivoting WHI-9 (display state optimization). Instead of deduping display state, pivot to a variant of multiref that is meant to be used internally by a node and tied to that node in the UI. This would let complex stateful nodes expose rich state without the serialization cost problem. Review the proposal, analyze feasibility, consider how it fits with existing multiref/ref architecture, and post a reply on WHI-9 Linear ticket via MCP tools. User says ideation phase needed.

title: research: propose Linear workflow statuses + audit all WHI tickets
description: Two parts: (1) Review current Linear statuses and propose a new workflow convention mapping to these stages: Initial Idea (not yet speced/researched), Pending Human Review (research done, awaiting owner answers to open questions), Ready to Implement (speced, approved, can be queued as task), In PR Review (code published as PR needing human review), Done. Create a Linear ticket (WHI-XX) proposing the convention — which Linear statuses to use/rename/add, how they map to the workflow. (2) After proposing, audit all open WHI tickets and recommend which status each should move to under the new convention. Post everything as a Linear ticket for human review/approval. Use MCP Linear tools.

title: research: review and answer WHI-16 comment on REPL plugin
description: User answered all 8 open questions on WHI-16 (remote REPL). Key decisions: (1) runtime agnostic, no Node.js sandbox — use eval() like Frostmod reference, (2) encrypt entire REPL transmission not just signature — use references/Frostmod as reference, (3) session-scoped REPL with access to globalThis, (4) no UI dependency concerns — use xterm.js, (5) generic plugin system, (6) public key as string hardcoded by user, (7) multi-line yes, (8) use Frostmod as reference for streaming. Review answers, check the Frostmod git submodule for reference implementation patterns, and post a reply on WHI-16 Linear ticket via MCP tools.
